reasoning: Chrome has dominated the browser market for over a decade, currently maintaining a share of around 65% across all platforms, compared to its nearest competitor (Safari) which sits at around 18-20%. Because of structural inertia, OS integration (Android), enterprise adoption, and user habits, it is virtually impossible for Chrome to lose its leading position by the end of 2025, regardless of regulatory challenges or minor shifts in market sentiment. Predicting any outcome other than a decisive victory for Chrome at the end of 2025 would require an unprecedented black-swan event such as the complete discontinuation of Chrome or global internet re-alignment.
